purpose: Machine-readable cross-reference maintenance framework for WellFit agents. It tells future agents which canonical registers, TODO files, Work Map entries, readiness files, validation scripts, and docs must be inspected or updated after route, API, product-module, agent-script, roadmap, feedback/insight, research, Unity/AR, compliance, or governance changes without creating duplicate architecture or parallel systems.

changeCategories.title: Route added or changed
changeCategories.trigger: A Next.js App Router page, visible route, page metadata, route group, navigation path, or route register entry changes.
forbiddenAutoUpdates: Do not auto-update native/unity/WellFitBuddyAR/** or PR #13 references.
Do not auto-update app/**, components/**, lib/**, functions/**, firestore.rules, or runtime product code from this framework.
Do not auto-update token, NFT, wallet, payment, betting, reward authority, health, child, location, privacy, legal, AGB, Datenschutz, Impressum, or compliance logic.
Do not create duplicate route, API, feature, product-readiness, roadmap, feedback, insight, research, or governance registries.
Do not replace historical TODO/status context; preserve it and add pointers only when useful.
humanReviewRules: Human review is required when a proposed cross-reference would assert unverified product behavior.
Human review is required when the change touches protected high/critical areas or would need runtime code to make the register true.
Human review is required before merge/deploy; this register is a maintenance checklist, not implementation approval.
examples: Adding /mobile/foo requires route/page register entries, feature/readiness links, visual smoke grouping when user-visible, and TODO/Work Map pointers if it creates a new maintenance surface.

changeCategories.title: API added or changed
changeCategories.trigger: An API route, endpoint contract, authority boundary, security metadata, or API register entry changes.

Do not replace historical TODO/status context; preserve it and add pointers only when useful.
Do not add API authority for rewards, payments, wallet, health, child, location, privacy, or compliance flows without explicit human approval.
humanReviewRules: Human review is required when a proposed cross-reference would assert unverified product behavior.
Human review is required when the change touches protected high/critical areas or would need runtime code to make the register true.
Human review is required before merge/deploy; this register is a maintenance checklist, not implementation approval.
examples: Changing an economy preview API requires API register authority/security metadata and explicit protected-area review; do not make client reward authority final.

humanReviewRequiredCategories: feature_or_product_module_changed when readiness advances a blocked/review-required/high-risk module
quality_gate_changed when a mandatory check is removed or relaxed

examples.action: Inspect route/page/feature/readiness/visual registers, update only canonical entries, then run route register, drift, visual, and quality-gate checks.
Add script pointer to Work Map and TODO Index, connect it to agent workflows/task queue/Autopilot when mandatory, integrate quality gate, then run the new script and quality gate.
Stop or keep planning-only; do not auto-fix runtime logic or legal/compliance text without explicit human approval.

kiContinuationPrompt: Lies zuerst AGENTS.md, todolist/CURRENT_PROJECT_STATE.md, todolist/WORK_MAP.md, todolist/TODO_INDEX.md, diese Datei und docs/architecture/WELLFIT_CROSS_REFERENCE_MAINTENANCE.md. Bestimme die Change Category, pruefe requiredInspectFiles, aktualisiere nur bestehende requiredUpdateTargets, vermeide Parallelarchitektur und fuehre node scripts/wellfit-dev-agent/src/cross-reference-maintenance-check.mjs sowie npm run agent:quality-gate aus.
